narration: An interval is a range with a start and an end, like [1, 4].
callout: Each pair tells where a range begins and ends.

narration: These two intervals overlap because the second one starts before the first one ends.
decision: If nextStart <= currentEnd, the intervals overlap.

choices.label: No, there is a gap
choices.feedback: Correct. 3 starts after 2 ends, so there is no shared space.

choices.label: Yes, they overlap
choices.feedback: Not this time. These intervals do not touch or share space.

narration: Sorting intervals by start time places them in a clean left-to-right order, which makes overlap easier to check.
callout: Sorting helps because possible overlap neighbors end up beside each other.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is left-to-right order so helpful for interval problems?
